(**NABE**) is the largest international association of applied economists, strategists, academics, and policy-makers committed to the application of economics.[[1]](#cite_note-Palgrave-1) Founded in 1959, it is one of the member organizations of the [Allied Social Science Associations](/wiki/Allied_Social_Science_Associations "Allied Social Science Associations"). According to the association's website, "NABE's mission is to provide leadership in the use and understanding of economics.".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

NABE offers [continuing education](/wiki/Continuing_education "Continuing education") through its Certified Business Economist program which features a comprehensive exam developed and administered by the association. Candidates must have at least a four-year degree, and two years of work experience in applied business economics or in a related field.

National Association for Business Economics

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Formation | 1959 |
| Purpose | [Business economics](/wiki/Business_economics "Business economics") |
| Headquarters | [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.") |
| Region served | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| Membership | 3,000[[1]](#cite_note-Palgrave-1) |
| Executive Director | Tom Beers |
| Website | <http://www.nabe.com> |

The **National Association for Business Economics** (**NABE**) is the largest international association of applied economists, strategists, academics, and policy-makers committed to the application of economics.[[1]](#cite_note-Palgrave-1) Founded in 1959, it is one of the member organizations of the [Allied Social Science Associations](/wiki/Allied_Social_Science_Associations "Allied Social Science Associations"). According to the association's website, "NABE's mission is to provide leadership in the use and understanding of economics.".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

NABE was holding its annual conference at the [Marriott World Trade Center](/wiki/Marriott_World_Trade_Center "Marriott World Trade Center") hotel during the [9/11](/wiki/9/11 "9/11") attacks.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The association's membership is divided into subject-oriented subdivisions or [round tables](/wiki/Round_table_(discussion) "Round table (discussion)"), including: financial, [health economics](/wiki/Health_economics "Health economics"), international, manufacturing, real estate/construction, regional/utility, small business/entrepreneurship, technology, and transfer pricing. Each round table plans and executes webinars and sessions at NABE meetings each year.[[5]](#cite_note-5) NABE also has local and student chapters[[6]](#cite_note-6) in many cities and much of the United States.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Leadership")]

### Presidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Presidents")]

Past presidents of the NABE include former [Chairman of the Federal Reserve](/wiki/Chairman_of_the_Federal_Reserve "Chairman of the Federal Reserve") [Alan Greenspan](/wiki/Alan_Greenspan "Alan Greenspan") and [Federal Reserve](/wiki/Federal_Reserve "Federal Reserve"), [Council of Economic Advisers](/wiki/Council_of_Economic_Advisers "Council of Economic Advisers") and [Congressional Budget Office](/wiki/Congressional_Budget_Office "Congressional Budget Office") advisor [Diane C. Swonk](/wiki/Diane_C._Swonk "Diane C. Swonk").[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) Since 2009, NABE's executive director has been Tom Beers, former chief economist of the [Manufactured Housing Institute](/wiki/Manufactured_Housing_Institute "Manufactured Housing Institute") and economist with the [National Association of Realtors](/wiki/National_Association_of_Realtors "National Association of Realtors") and the [Bureau of Labor Statistics](/wiki/Bureau_of_Labor_Statistics "Bureau of Labor Statistics").

Past presidents include:[[10]](#cite_note-NABEpp-10)

| Name | Term | Position |
| --- | --- | --- |
| George W. McKinney Jr. | 1965 – 1966 | Senior vice president, [Irving Trust Company](/wiki/Irving_Trust_Company "Irving Trust Company") |
| William Chartener | 1967 – 1968 | Chief economist, [Goldman, Sachs & Co.](/wiki/Goldman,_Sachs_%26_Co. "Goldman, Sachs & Co.") |
| [Alan Greenspan](/wiki/Alan_Greenspan "Alan Greenspan") | 1969 – 1970 | Co-founder, Townsend, Greenspan & Co. |
| [Diane C. Swonk](/wiki/Diane_C._Swonk "Diane C. Swonk") | 1999 – 2000 | Chief economist, [KPMG](/wiki/KPMG "KPMG") |
| [Ellen Hughes-Cromwick](/wiki/Ellen_Hughes-Cromwick "Ellen Hughes-Cromwick") | 2007 – 2008 | Chief economist, [Ford Motor Company](/wiki/Ford_Motor_Company "Ford Motor Company") |
| Chris Varvares | 2008 – 2009 | President, Macroeconomic Advisers |
| Lynn Reaser | 2009 – 2010 | Chief economist, [Point Loma Nazarene University](/wiki/Point_Loma_Nazarene_University "Point Loma Nazarene University") |
| Richard L. Wobbekind | 2010 – 2011 | Director of business research division and associate dean for MBA and Enterprise Programs at the University of Colorado at Boulder |
| Gene Huang | 2011 – 2012 | Vice president & chief economist, [FedEx](/wiki/FedEx "FedEx") |
| Ken Simonson | 2012 – 2013 | Chief economist, [Associated General Contractors of America](/wiki/Associated_General_Contractors_of_America "Associated General Contractors of America") |
| Jack Kleinhenx | 2013 – 2014 | Chief economist, [National Retail Federation](/wiki/National_Retail_Federation "National Retail Federation") |
| John Silvia | 2014 – 2015 | Managing director and chief economist, [Wells Fargo](/wiki/Wells_Fargo "Wells Fargo") |
| Lisa Emsbo-Mattingly | 2015 – 2016 | Research director, asset allocation, [Fidelity Investments](/wiki/Fidelity_Investments "Fidelity Investments") |
| [Stuart P. M. Mackintosh](/wiki/Stuart_P._M._Mackintosh "Stuart P. M. Mackintosh") | 2016 – 2017 | Executive director, [Group of Thirty](/wiki/Group_of_Thirty "Group of Thirty") |
| Mine Yucel | 2017 – 2018 | Senior vice president and research director, [Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas](/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Bank_of_Dallas "Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas") |
| Kevin Swift | 2018 – 2019 | Chief economist, [American Chemistry Council](/wiki/American_Chemistry_Council "American Chemistry Council") |
| [Constance Hunter](/wiki/Constance_Hunter "Constance Hunter") | 2019 – 2020 | Chief economist, [AIG](/wiki/AIG "AIG")[[11]](#cite_note-11) and, formerly, [KPMG](/wiki/KPMG "KPMG") |
| Manuel Balmaseda | 2020 – 2021 | Chief economist, [CEMEX](/wiki/CEMEX "CEMEX");[[10]](#cite_note-NABEpp-10) adjunct professor, [IE University](/wiki/IE_University "IE University")[[12]](#cite_note-NABEbOd-12) |
| David E. Altig | 2021 – 2022 | Executive vice president and director of research, [Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta](/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Bank_of_Atlanta "Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta")[[12]](#cite_note-NABEbOd-12) |
| Julia Coronado | 2022 – 2023 | President and Founder, MacroPolicy Perspectives LLC[[13]](#cite_note-13) |

## Activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Activities")]

* NABE Annual Meeting: The NABE Annual Meeting features speakers, panel discussions, educational sessions, and networking opportunities. In addition to NABE Members and those in related fields, attendees include business leaders, government officials, analysts, investors, and members of the business media.
* Economic Policy Conference: Held annually in the spring in [Washington, DC](/wiki/Washington,_DC "Washington, DC"), focuses "on the one seemingly-unpredictable variable that regularly confounds many economists and their models: economic policy". Speakers at the Economic Policy Conference typically include leaders from the [Federal Reserve Board](/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Board "Federal Reserve Board"), Treasury Department, [Congressional Budget Office](/wiki/Congressional_Budget_Office "Congressional Budget Office"), [Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation](/wiki/Federal_Deposit_Insurance_Corporation "Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation"), and other government agencies, in addition to analysts at [think tanks](/wiki/Think_tank "Think tank") and other institutions.
* Webinars/Teleconferences: Each of the ten NABE Roundtables conducts regular [webinars](/wiki/Webinar "Webinar") and [teleconferences](/wiki/Teleconference "Teleconference") on topics within its area of interest. These sessions are available to both members and non-members.
* Publications: NABE offers a variety of publications. *Business Economics*, its journal - as below - is best known of these.
* NABE Surveys: NABE regularly conducts three [surveys of its membership](/wiki/Survey_methodology "Survey methodology"): the *Outlook Survey*, the *Industry Survey*, and the *Economic Policy Survey*. The NABE Outlook Survey, the most well-known of the NABE surveys, provides the consensus forecast of NABE’s professional [economic forecasters](/wiki/Economic_forecast "Economic forecast").

## Education and career development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education and career development")]

* **Certified Business Economist**: NABE offers [continuing education](/wiki/Continuing_education "Continuing education") through its Certified Business Economist program which features a comprehensive exam developed and administered by the association. Candidates must have at least a four-year degree, and two years of work experience in applied business economics or in a related field.
* Econometrics Certificate Programs: Offered twice yearly at Federal Reserve Banks, NABE's [Econometrics](/wiki/Econometrics "Econometrics") Seminars are among its offerings. Each Summer, NABE offers the **Certificate in Applied Econometrics**, and each Fall, the **Certificate in Time-Series Analysis and Forecasting**.
* NABE career site: caters to those seeking a job or an employee in the field of economics.
* No accreditation appears to exists for their educational products or services.

## Journal publication

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Journal publication")]

NABE is the publisher of *Business Economics*, a [scholarly journal](/wiki/Academic_journal "Academic journal") that covers different aspects of [applied economics](/wiki/Applied_economics "Applied economics") and is published quarterly.[[14]](#cite_note-14) The journal serves as an essential resource and provides practical information for people who apply economics in the workplace. It is the leading forum for debating solutions to critical business problems, analyzing key business and economic issues, and sharing of best-practice models, tools, and hands-on techniques from practitioners in the field of economics.

## Adam Smith Award

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Adam Smith Award")]

Since 1982, NABE has awarded the [Adam Smith](/wiki/Adam_Smith "Adam Smith") Award[[15]](#cite_note-15) to prominent economists and policy makers who have contributed to business economics. Recipients of the award include former [Federal Reserve](/wiki/Federal_Reserve "Federal Reserve") chairman [Ben Bernanke](/wiki/Ben_Bernanke "Ben Bernanke"), in 2014; CEO and president of [TIAA-CREF](/wiki/TIAA-CREF "TIAA-CREF") [Roger Ferguson](/wiki/Roger_Ferguson "Roger Ferguson"), in 2013; vice chair of the [Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System](/wiki/Board_of_Governors_of_the_Federal_Reserve_System "Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System") [Janet Yellen](/wiki/Janet_Yellen "Janet Yellen"), in 2010;[[16]](#cite_note-16) former director of the [National Economic Council](/wiki/United_States_National_Economic_Council "United States National Economic Council") [Lawrence Summers](/wiki/Lawrence_Summers "Lawrence Summers") in 2009, chief executive of the [Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis](/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Bank_of_St._Louis "Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis") [William Poole](/wiki/William_Poole "William Poole"), in 2006; [Princeton University](/wiki/Princeton_University "Princeton University") economics professor [Paul Krugman](/wiki/Paul_Krugman "Paul Krugman"), in 1995; and [Chicago School](/wiki/Chicago_school_of_economics "Chicago school of economics") economist and recipient of the [Nobel Prize in Economics](/wiki/Nobel_Memorial_Prize_in_Economic_Sciences "Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences") [Milton Friedman](/wiki/Milton_Friedman "Milton Friedman") in 1989.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Paul A. Volcker Lifetime Achievement Award for Economic Policy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Association_for_Business_Economics&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Paul A. Volcker Lifetime Achievement Award for Economic Policy")]

Each March, NABE presents the [Paul A. Volcker](/wiki/Paul_A._Volcker "Paul A. Volcker") Award[[18]](#cite_note-18) to a senior policymaker recognizing outstanding contributions to the field of economic and monetary policy in his or her career. Past recipients include:

| Year | NABE |
| --- | --- |
| 2013 | Paul A. Volcker |
| 2014 | Jean-Claude Trichet |
| 2015 | Alice Rivlin |
| 2016 | Stanley Fischer |
| 2017 | Joseph Stiglitz |
| 2018 | Mervyn King |
| 2019 | Alan Greenspan |
| 2020 | Roger Ferguson |
